reasoning: The informal statement asks for the value of the infinite product $\prod_{n=2}^{\infty} \frac{n^3-1}{n^3+1}$. The Lean formalization encodes this as the limit of the partial products $\prod_{n=2}^{N} \frac{n^3-1}{n^3+1}$ as $N \to \infty$ (via `Tendsto ... atTop (𝓝 (2/3))`), which is the standard definition of an infinite product of real numbers. Using `Finset.Icc (2 : ℤ) N` indexed over integers yields the same partial product values as indexing over natural numbers from 2 to N. The closed-form answer 2/3 is correct (telescoping gives $\frac{2(N^2+N+1)}{3N(N+1)} \to 2/3$). No hypotheses are added, dropped, or altered, and no quantifier structure is changed.
